mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance relative to baseline during rate cuts, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in over 65% of instances following a 0.5pp unemployment spike, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE exceeds 7%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized domestic demand growth, producing positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS growth exceeds 5% annually while HOUST declines, or interaction term IC remains negative over 5-year samples.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in the index of industrial production reflect strengthening manufacturing output and corporate earnings momentum, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during periods of high inflation, or the signal reverses direction when industrial capacity utilization exceeds 85%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above the 2.5% threshold forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in over 70% of high-inflation periods, or the inflation threshold shows no statistical relationship with forward equity performance.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month through earnings optimism.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal predicts negative returns when wage growth outpaces productivity, or the interaction effect vanishes when PAYEMS growth falls below 100k monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product establishes a baseline of economic expansion that supports corporate revenue expectations, yielding positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows zero correlation with 5-day forward returns across multiple business cycles, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 4-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly contractions in industrial production signal a supply-side contraction and weakening business investment, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in over 60% of instances following dual-month industrial declines, or the signal loses predictive accuracy when global commodity prices surge.
